purpose: Capabilities currently executable by the Unity NPC runtime.

runtime_contract.planner_role: Convert user intent into ordered steps using only executable_actions.
runtime_contract.unity_role: Resolve scene objects, validate existence, and execute final actions.
runtime_contract.target_resolution: Use object names or aliases in steps. Unity resolves them into object_id values before execution.

executable_actions.description: Move the NPC to a scene object or location.
Move to an item and pick it up.
Put down one inventory item into the world near the NPC.
examples: place the apple on the ground

executable_actions.description: Stop the NPC's current queued or active actions.

planning_rules: Only use executable_actions when producing steps.
Use get_item for direct pickup requests.
Use put_item for requests to put down, drop, or place an inventory item in the world.
For compound requests, produce one step per requested executable action in order.

If the user's goal cannot be completed using only executable_actions, do not invent Unity commands. Return action null, steps empty, and explain that the required Unity capability is not available yet.
Keep target names concise. Use the object or place name only, without generic words such as location, place, position, near, or around.
Translate all command field values into English.
